name: Planet (fleet)

overview.value: Planet was founded with the mission to image the Earth every day and make change visible, accessible, and actionable. The company designs, builds, and operates satellite constellations that deliver detailed, high-frequency data used by governments, companies, and researchers to track daily changes at a global scale. Planet says it has built and successfully deployed 450 satellites and currently operates over 200 in orbit.

mcc_read.text: Planet's real product is time: an archive back to 2009 and a near-daily scan of the whole landmass since 2017 that no amount of rival capex can backfill. The soft spot is sharpness: 3 m daily pixels cede the high-detail tasking market, and SkySat plus the 30 cm Pelican line are the admission.

notes: Company page states 200+ satellites in orbit and 450 built and deployed; 200 recorded as the stated floor. Constellations named: SuperDove, SkySat, Pelican, Tanager, Owl. Weekly maintenance (2026-07-23), EO spec backfill attempted and declined: this profile is the umbrella fleet parent spanning heterogeneous sub-constellations (Dove/SuperDove ~3-5 m optical, SkySat ~50 cm, Pelican, Tanager hyperspectral), each already carrying or slated for its own dedicated profile (superdove.json, skysat.json, pelican.json, tanager.json). eoPortal's 'planet' (Flock/Dove) mission page states GSD as ranges only ('2.7-3.2 m' / '3.7-4.9 m' nadir, both orbit-type dependent) and its 3-5 m SuperDove figure is already the sourced basis for superdove.json's resolution_m; writing a fleet-wide resolution_m/swath_km/spectral_bands here would either duplicate or conflate distinct sub-fleet figures already owned by child profiles, so those fields are left null, matching the design already documented on the radarsat.json fleet parent. The same eoPortal page states a design target to 'maintain 100-150 satellite constellation'; left out of sats_planned because the field takes a single number and this is a stated range, and because it is ambiguous whether it covers the whole company fleet or only the Sun-synchronous Dove/SuperDove line.

events.headline: Planet's Flock-1 constellation launches aboard Antares to the ISS
Planet acquires BlackBridge and its RapidEye constellation
Planet to acquire Terra Bella and its SkySat constellation from Google
Planet Labs PBC begins trading on NYSE under ticker PL after SPAC merger
Planet acquires Sinergise, expanding its Earth Data Platform
Planet launches Tanager-1, its first hyperspectral satellite
